item.title: GoPro under intense pressure from rising costs and competition
item.description: GoPro cameras have enabled the adventurous to record images of their experiences for nearly 25 years. But the company is under extreme pressure from intensifying competition, rising costs and more.

item.title: Woman faces murder charges in killing of U.S. diplomat in Myanmar
item.description: The diplomat was found dead at the Sakura Residence & Hotel, according to attorneys familiar with the case.

item.title: U.S. denies Iran has closed Strait of Hormuz as war reignites
item.description: The U.S. denies Iran's claim to have closed the Strait of Hormuz amid a new exchange of fire, and India fumes over a U.S. strike that killed 3 tanker crewmembers.

item.title: Poll: Most believe extraterrestrial life exists – and some think it's already here
item.description: Many have watched recently released UFO videos, but most still think the government knows more than it is saying.

item.title: What will happen to gold prices if inflation stays elevated through 2026? Here's what experts say.
item.description: What impact will the latest inflation surge have on gold prices? Here's what some experts are predicting now.

item.title: 5 police officers shot dead in Mexico as World Cup kicks off
item.description: Five Mexican police officers were killed and five others wounded on the eve of the World Cup opener in Mexico City, authorities said.

item.title: Knicks rally from 29 points down to beat Spurs 107-106
item.description: The Knicks set the record for the biggest NBA Finals comeback Wednesday night as they rallied from 29 points down to beat the Spurs 107-106.

item.title: House to take up last-minute extension of FISA spy power in likely-doomed vote
item.description: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act lapses Friday.

item.title: How FIFA World Cup rules on extra time and tiebreakers work for 2026
item.description: Games are likely to be tied at the end of regulation at the 2026 World Cup, especially in the late stages of the tournament with a highly competitive field.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off with Shakira at 1st of 3 opening ceremonies
item.description: With matches being played in 11 cities across the U.S., Mexico and Canada​, fans are getting three World Cup opening ceremonies.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: FOX and NBCUniversal​ have the broadcasting rights for the 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico.

item.title: When the U.S. men's soccer team is playing in the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team, which last appeared at the 2022 World Cup, will face Paraguay to kick off its 2026 World Cup.

item.title: U.S., Iran trade attacks again after Trump threat
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire again on Thursday after President Trump pledged Tehran would "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.

item.title: Here's who will face off in California's new House districts as Dems seek edge
item.description: California's Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom spearheaded a redistricting initiative that was intended to make up to five more districts more friendly to Democrats. Voters may have different ideas.

item.title: Is China's Xi worried that North Korea wants "to confront the U.S."?
item.description: A veteran North Korea analyst says Xi Jinping's visit to North Korea was about keeping tabs on an emboldened Kim Jong Un who wants "to confront the U.S."

item.title: Vance Boelter expected to plead guilty to federal charges
item.description: Vance Boelter, the man accused of a deadly attack on Minnesota lawmakers, is expected to plead guilty to federal charges Thursday morning.

item.title: U.S. launches more strikes on Iranian targets after Trump promises to hit Iran "hard"
item.description: The U.S. military launched an additional round of strikes on targets within Iran early Thursday morning local time, hours after President Trump vowed to hit Iran "hard."

item.title: Belfast protests erupt again after stabbing as police deploy water cannons
item.description: Police have used water cannons on protesters in Northern Ireland after violence erupted for a second night over a stabbing in Belfast.

item.title: Details on new U.S. strikes against Iran
item.description: U.S. Central Command said the U.S. launched a new round of what it called self-defense strikes against Iran on Wednesday. Two U.S. officials familiar with the strikes told CBS News the targets include amminuition depots, command and control centers and warehouses. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Courtney Kealy have more.

item.title: Breaking down Maine, South Carolina primary results
item.description: Despite a string of recently revealed controversies surrounding his past, Graham Platner won the Democratic primary for Maine's Senate seat on Tuesday night. He'll face off against incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more on this and South Carolina's gubernatorial primary results.

item.title: Bill Gates tells Congress that meeting Epstein was "a grave error in judgment"
item.description: Bill Gates told members of Congress on Wednesday that Jeffrey Epstein put his philanthropic work at risk, and that meeting him represented "a grave error in judgment."

item.title: Inflation topped 4% in May as CPI surged to highest level in 3 years
item.description: The Consumer Price Index rose last month at a 4.2% annual rate amid a spike in U.S. energy prices.

item.title: Meta launches program to train workers for data center jobs
item.description: Meta pledged to invest $115 million to train electricians, plumbers and other workers needed to operate data centers.

item.title: Florida Supreme Court keeps redistricted congressional map in place
item.description: In a 6-1 ruling, the court determined the First District Court of Appeal should consider the merits of the case before it weighs in on the matter.

item.title: Trump says "I love the inflation" because U.S. is "taking out" Iranian oil
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. has taken out "millions" of barrels of Iranian oil in the dead of night, and said inflation will come down when the war ends.

item.title: America's birth rate has plunged. Are smartphones to blame?
item.description: The iPhone was introduced in 2007, the same year the U.S. birth rate started to slide. The issues could be linked, a new analysis finds.

item.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
item.description: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

item.title: U.S. Soccer unveils new stars and stripes jerseys ahead of 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team is expected to debut the new uniforms later this month in Atlanta, ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup starting in June.

item.title: U.S. cities will see big tourism boost from World Cup, analysis predicts
item.description: After a lackluster year for tourism, the U.S. is likely to see a jump in international visitors for the 2026 World Cup, economists say.
